data.title: Bitcoin treasury firm Nakamoto slides nearly 10% after name change announcement
data.description:
Nakamoto shares fell nearly 10% after the company announced a name change from KindlyMD.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/nakamoto-slides-7-after-name-change/

data.title: The United Nations received funding from Circle to advance the digital upgrade of cross-border refugee assistance.
data.description: Stablecoin issuer Circle announced a grant to the United Nations to support its global deployment of digital financial infrastructure to improve the efficiency of humanitarian aid disbursement. The announcement was made during the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland. The Circle Foundation stated that this is its first international grant and will support the UN Centre for Digital Solutions in Fiscal Affairs (DHoTS) in its work to streamline cross-border fund transfers within the UN system. This grant continues Circle's 2022 collaboration with the UNHCR and DHoTS, when they disbursed aid to displaced persons in Ukraine via the USDC stablecoin. UNDP Administrator Alexander De Croo stated that stablecoin payments help “make every dollar more effective” in a budget-constrained environment. Circle noted that approximately $38 billion in humanitarian aid currently relies on the traditional financial system annually, and digital financial infrastructure promises to significantly improve the efficiency of fund utilization. UNHCR High Commissioner Barham Salih also stated that the project aims to enhance the dignity and choices of forcibly displaced persons through technological means, while maximizing the actual impact of donated funds.

data.title: Circle Funds UN Initiative to Enhance Humanitarian Aid with Digital Finance
data.description: Circle has allocated funds to the United Nations to enhance digital financial infrastructure, aiming to improve the efficiency of humanitarian aid distribution. According to NS3.AI, this grant will support the UN's Digital Hub for Treasury Solutions in optimizing cross-border fund transfers. This initiative builds on Circle's previous collaboration with the UN Refugee Agency, where USDC was utilized for aid in Ukraine. The project underscores the potential of stablecoin payments to make the distribution of approximately $38 billion in annual humanitarian aid more efficient and cost-effective.

data.title: The Senate Agriculture Committee released the latest text of the cryptocurrency market structure bill, failing to reach an agreement with Democrats.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett wrote that the extra two weeks of negotiations to secure formal Democratic support failed to yield results, and next week's Senate Agriculture Committee (@SenateAg) markup is expected to be partisan, which is not a positive sign for the Senate Banking Committee, which had hoped to advance its review with bipartisan consensus. She further stated that the Senate Agriculture Committee has released the latest version of the cryptocurrency market structure bill text, and Committee Chairman John Boozman indicated that no agreement had been reached with the Democrats, but he still looks forward to next week's review meeting. The meeting is scheduled for next Tuesday at 3 PM Eastern Time.

data.title: Bitmine bought another 34,954 ETH, worth $105.5 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, in the past two hours, Bitmine (0x1E1...c89F), owned by Tom Lee (@fundstrat), bought 34,954 ETH from Kraken and BitGo, worth $105.5 million.

data.title: A trader's $332 million short position was liquidated, with one-third of the position being liquidated and $20 million in unrealized profits being written off.
data.description: According to Ember Monitoring, at 3:00 AM today, a market rebound caused one-third of the $332 million short position held by a trader (0xD83...Fd7) to be liquidated. The trader's unrealized profit decreased from $24 million to $4 million, a loss of $20 million. After the position was liquidated, the trader added more short positions and currently holds short positions worth $266 million.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Shows Increase in Performance
data.description: The Altcoin Season Index has experienced a rise, increasing by four points to reach 32. According to NS3.AI, this index assesses the performance of altcoins relative to Bitcoin by analyzing the price trends of the top 100 cryptocurrencies, excluding stablecoins and wrapped coins. An altcoin season is declared when 75% of these altcoins outperform Bitcoin over a span of 90 days.

data.title: Aave Protocol Integrated by Canada's Largest Digital Asset Custodian Balance
data.description: Canada's leading digital asset custodian, Balance, has integrated the Aave protocol, according to PANews. Balance clients can now utilize Aave for lending and earning returns on assets held in their offline or hot wallets.

data.title: Vietnam opens crypto exchange licensing with nearly $400 million capital barrier to entry
data.description:
The pilot regime favors domestic banks and securities firms, with ownership rules and capital requirements likely limiting participation.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/386543/vietnam-opens-crypto-exchange-licensing?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: BNB Surpasses 890 USDT with a 1.89% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 22, 2026, 00:32 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 890 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 890.039978 USDT, with a narrowed 1.89% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: OpenAI Seeks Major Funding from Middle Eastern Investors
data.description: OpenAI is reportedly in discussions with top investors in the Middle East, including Abu Dhabi's sovereign fund, to secure at least $50 billion in new funding. According to PANews, the company aims for a valuation between $750 billion and $830 billion. The funds are intended to cover the high costs associated with developing its AI systems, including expenses for chips, data centers, and talent. OpenAI has previously collaborated with Abu Dhabi investment entities MGX and G42 and plans to invest over $1.4 trillion in AI infrastructure over the coming years. The current funding round is still in its early stages, and the amount may be subject to change.

data.title: The United States formally withdraws from the World Health Organization.
data.description: On January 20th last year, US President Trump signed an executive order announcing the United States' withdrawal from the World Health Organization (WHO), and the United Nations received this notification on January 22nd of the same year. According to the WHO's charter, a member state can only formally withdraw one year after submitting a withdrawal application. Today marks one year since the US formally submitted its application to the United Nations to withdraw from the WHO, meaning that the US has officially withdrawn from the WHO procedurally. (CCTV)

data.title: Brother Machi used 25x leverage to add to his long position of $10 million in ETH.
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on the X platform that "Brother Maji" (0x020...5872) has deposited another $419,700 USDC into HyperLiquid to increase his 25x leveraged long position in ETH. This position is worth $10 million and holds 3,333.88 ETH.

data.title: Uniswap: Continuous clearing auctions have been officially deployed to Base.
data.description: Uniswap announced on its X platform that continuous liquidation auctions have been officially deployed to the Base network. Base developers can now fully run on-chain token auctions, discover reliable market prices, and launch liquidity on Uniswap v4.

data.title: The decentralized social protocol Farcaster will be handed over to Neynar for operation.
data.description: Dan Romero, co-founder of the decentralized social protocol Farcaster, announced that Neynar is acquiring Farcaster. Over the next few weeks, ownership of Farcaster's protocol contracts and code repository, the Farcaster application, and Clanker will be transferred to Neynar, which will be responsible for the continued operation and maintenance of the related products and infrastructure. Romero stated that some members of the Merkle team, Varun, and himself will step down from Farcaster's day-to-day operations to focus on new projects. He noted that the Neynar team has been deeply involved in Farcaster's ecosystem since its inception, being one of Farcaster's earliest clients, and its infrastructure currently supports most of the developer ecosystem. Romero stated that after five years of development, Farcaster needs a new development path and leadership to unlock its potential, and expressed anticipation for the direction Neynar will take after the acquisition.

data.title: A major ETH whale bought another 10,000 ETH, realizing a profit of $973,000.
data.description: According to AiYi's monitoring, "smart money" (0xFB7...e0A3), which profited $98.18 million from ETH trading, received 10,000 ETH from Wintermute near the bottom of the ETH price drop 6 hours ago, worth approximately $28.96 million, suspected to be an OTC purchase. Since yesterday, this address has added 30,000 ETH, worth approximately $88.37 million, with an average cost of approximately $2,945.7, and currently has a floating profit of $973,000.

data.title: European Stock Futures Rise Amid Market Optimism
data.description: European stock futures experienced an upward trend, with the Euro Stoxx 50 futures increasing by 1.3%. According to ChainCatcher, Germany's DAX futures also saw a rise of 1.3%. This movement reflects a positive sentiment in the European markets.

data.title: Canada’s Carney faces pressure at home over EV trade truce with China
data.description:
Ontario Premier Doug Ford has called on Canada to boycott Chinese-made electric vehicles (EVs) that will be entering the country as a result of the recent deal the federal government struck with China. Speaking at Queen’s Park on Wednesday, January 21, Ford issued his strongest condemnation yet of the agreement, which allows up to 49,000 […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/canada-carney-pressure-ev-trade-truce-china/

data.title: F/m Investments has applied to tokenize its ETF shares, aiming to become the first issuer to advance ETF tokenization.
data.description: F/m Investments, an ETF issuer with approximately $18 billion in assets under management, has applied to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for regulatory approval to tokenize its ETF shares and record them on a permissioned blockchain ledger. The product the company plans to tokenize is its 3-month U.S. Treasury Bill ETF (TBIL, Nasdaq: TBIL). F/m Investments stated that this is the first time an institution has explicitly applied to the SEC for an exemption regarding the tokenization of ETF shares of a registered investment company. The company stated that if approved, existing TBIL ETF shares will be represented on-chain on the permissioned ledger using the same CUSIP code, and will remain consistent with existing ETF shares in terms of rights, fees, voting rights, and economic terms, still subject to the Investment Company Act of 1940. The company also emphasized that the arrangement will retain board oversight, daily disclosure, and third-party custody and auditing mechanisms. F/m Investments CEO Alexander Morris stated that the tokenization trend of traditional financial instruments is inevitable. (The Block)
